channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ownership group of NBA champion Boston Celtics is putting team up for sale
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The ownership group that controls the reigning NBA champion Boston Celtics says it intends to sell all its shares of the team. In a statement released Monday, Boston Basketball Partners LLC said it intends to sell the majority interest of the team in 2024 or early 2025.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O.J. Simpson included with Willy Mays in awkward awards show tribute
item.description: There was 'stunned silence' when Simpson's photo appeared on screen during the 'In Memoriam' segment at the BET Awards Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Klay Thompson could be close to joining Dallas Mavericks, according to reports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Klay Thompson could be planning to join the Dallas Mavericks on a three-year, $50 million deal with a player option, according to ESPN.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Real Salt Lake duo voted onto MLS All-Star Roster for July showcase
item.description: For the first time in nearly 10 years, Real Salt Lake will have two players represent the club among the best in the league in the MLS All-Star game on July 24.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Nashville Predators sign Stamkos, Marchessault, Skjei in free agency frenzy; Guentzel signs in Tampa
item.description: The Nashville Predators made a big splash as NHL free agency opened Monday by signing Stanley Cup champions Steven Stamkos and Jonathan Marchessault in a jaw-dropping series of moves topping $100 million that made Smashville the center of attention across hockey.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL great Shannon Sharpe reveals why he refuses to sleep at women's homes
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Famer Shannon Sharpe talked to ex-NFL star Cam Newton and revealed why he refuses to sleep at another woman's house. He pointed to two incidents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennis star Aryna Sabalenka withdraws from Wimbledon
item.description: No. 3 seed in the Wimbledon tennis championships, Aryna Sabalenka, withdrew from the tournament due to a shoulder injury as Wimbledon started on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bland wins rain-delayed U.S. Senior Open on 4th playoff hole for his second straight senior major
item.description: NEWPORT, R.I. (AP) — Richard Bland shot a 4-under 66 in the final round of the U.S. Senior Open, catching leader Hiroyuki Fujita after rain postponed the finish to Monday morning and then banging a chip shot off the pin to beat him on the fourth playoff hole and win his second straight senior major.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bland wins rain-delayed U.S. Senior Open on 4th playoff hole for his second straight senior major
item.description: Richard Bland shot a 4-under 66 in the final round of the U.S. Senior Open and banged a chip shot off the pin on the fourth playoff hole to win his second straight senior major.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lightning's Steven Stamkos bids farewell to Tampa, joins Predators on 4-year, $32M deal
item.description: Lightning's franchise star player, former No. 1 overall draft pick and captain Steven Stamkos bid farewell to the Bolts just as the NHL free agency began on Monday despite the team's general manager saying his resigning was a "top priority.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Jose Sharks add scoring forward in free agency
item.description: San Jose Sharks have bolstered their forward group on the first day of NHL free agency, signing Tyler Toffoli
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Morgan Scalley named Utah's 'head coach in waiting'
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Whenever Kyle Whittingham decides to retire, the Utes football program already have its new head coach ready to take over. Utah athletic director Mark Harlan announced on Monday that defensive coordinator Morgan Scalley is Utah's "head coach in waiting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz sign Suns center Drew Eubanks to two-year deal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Jazz CEO Danny Ainge vowed to make a big splash this off-season by going "big game hunting," but their first free agent signing was more of a ripple.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rookie sensation Angel Reese sets WNBA record, but Chicago Sky fall at home to Minnesota Lynx
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The splendid rookie season of Chicago Sky forward Angel Reese has now resulted in a WNBA record. Reese tallied 10 points and 16 rebounds against the Minnesota Lynx on Sunday at Wintrust Arena, marking her 10th straight double-double.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Logano wins at Nashville for 1st NASCAR Cup Series victory of the year
item.description: Joey Logano won the rain-delayed race at Nashville Superspeedway in a NASCAR Cup Series-record fifth overtime Sunday night, stretching his fuel enough to hold off a group of pursuers at the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Hawkins, Fox 5

item.title: Brazil set for NorCal return in Copa America 30 years after South Bay takeover
item.description: Brazil’s arrival to the region will no doubt bring back memories of the 1994 World Cup, when legions of Seleção fans took over mainstreet Los Gatos during the early stages of the iconic team’s run to the championship on United States soil.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Before the Pac-12 disappears: Our picks for the greatest players in conference history (the all-time team)
item.description: The conference will cease to exist as we know it on Aug. 2. Before it does, the Hotline selected the greatest players across the century.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Alcaraz opens Wimbledon with straight-set win, Sabalenka and Azarenka withdraw with injuries
item.description: Defending champion Carlos Alcaraz had goosebumps walking onto Centre Court before his opening match at Wimbledon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rockies take final game against White Sox, Jackalopes tie in final game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — It has been a week since the Grand Junction Jackalopes won a game. After losing the last five games to the Rocky Mountain Vibes, the Lopes looked to close out the series with a win.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: 7v7 Football: Torrey Pines 14, West Hills 0
item.description: In the "Battle on the Mountain" 7v7 Football Tournament at Ramona High School, Torrey Pines defeated West Hills 14-0.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Toglia hits a sacrifice fly in the 14th inning as the Rockies top the White Sox 5-4
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ryan McMahon scored on Michael Toglia's sacrifice fly in the 14th inning, and the Colorado Rockies stopped a five-game slide by topping the Chicago White Sox 5-4 on Sunday. McMahon began the 14th on second as the automatic runner. He advanced on Sam Hillard's sacrifice bunt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors waive Chris Paul: reports
item.description: (KRON) – The Golden State Warriors have waived veteran Chris Paul on Sunday, making him a free agent, according to multiple reports. The guard had a $30 million guarantee deal with the Warriors. Now that Paul is a free agent, he has reportedly signed with another team.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulls out of U.S. Olympic trials with leg injury
item.description: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulled out of the U.S. Olympic trials, one day after injuring her left knee while training on the vault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay Lightning acquire the rights to Jake Guentzel on the eve of free agency
item.description: The Lightning sent a 2025 third-round draft pick to the Carolina Hurricanes. The move allows the Lightning to sign Guentzel before he hits the open market on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: The escalator's malfunction resulted “in an increased downward speed,” the Brewers said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: NFL violates antitrust law, Nuggets eye Klay Thompson
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — An eight-person jury ruled in favor of the plaintiffs, ordering the league to pay $14.39 billion in damages. This case centers on the offering of out-of-market Sunday games through a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Ahead of the game against the Houston Astros, Mets fans cheer on their team
item.description: FLUSHING, Queens (PIX11) — The Mets winning streak came to an end Saturday after facing a tough loss against the Houston Astros. PIX11 News caught up with energized fans at a block party, before the game, who were hopeful for another win.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, PIX 11

item.title: Could this be the end of the Kawhi Leonard-Paul George era for the Los Angeles Clippers?
item.description: Los Angeles Clippers star Paul George will become an NBA free agent after he opted out of the final year of his contract with the team, according to a report. ESPN's Adrian Wojnarowski reported on Saturday that the 34-year-old declined the option worth $48.7 million.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: New Orleans Saints wide receiver Chris Olave returns to Mission Hills for 3rd annual youth camp
item.description: New Orleans Saints Wide Receiver and Mission Hills alum Chris Olave returns back home to host his 3rd annual youth camp at his alma mater.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL QB, JaMarcus Russell, sued over $74k donation intended for alma mater
item.description: Former NFL No. 1 overall draft pick and LSU standout quarterback JaMarcus Russell is facing a lawsuit over a $74,000 check written as a donation for the football program at his alma mater, Williamson High School.
item.dc:creator: Simone Eli, NewsNation

item.title: Giants great, Hall of Famer Orlando Cepeda dies at 86
item.description: (KRON) -- San Francisco Giants great and baseball Hall of Famer Orlando Cepeda died at the age of 86, the Giants announced Friday. The Giants honored Cepeda with a moment of silence at Oracle Park during their Friday night game against the Dodgers. His death comes 10 days after Willie Mays died.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Mets' unexpected success in 19-game run boosts wildcard hopes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Why do we watch sports? Distraction, great athletes, team allegiances. Repeat after me: sports are fun. It is great to watch elite-level athletes playing a game at a level that you and I could only dream of. It brings people together, as well as families and friends.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Luke Combs takes the wheel at Ally 400 NASCAR race in Nashville
item.description: Country music superstar Luke Combs will swap his guitar for driving gloves as the honorary pace car driver at the Ally 400 NASCAR Cup Series event on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8B in 'Sunday Ticket' case
item.description: The lawsuit claimed the league broke antitrust laws by selling its package of Sunday games at an inflated price.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8 billion in 'Sunday Ticket' case for violating antitrust laws
item.description: A jury in U.S. District Court ordered the NFL to pay nearly $4.8 billion in damages Thursday after ruling that the league violated antitrust laws in distributing out-of-market Sunday afternoon games on a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: FIFA World Cup 26 will generate more than half a billion dollars for Los Angeles: study
item.description: The storylines in store for soccer fans ahead of FIFA World Cup 26 are already sizzling — a potential retirement sendoff for legends like Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, an expanded field of 48 teams and likely the most talented team in United States Men's National Team history — but the tournam
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: NBA Draft: Two CU Buffaloes are selected, Nuggets grab big man
item.description: The NBA Draft's first round is in the books as not one but two Colorado Buffaloes heard their names called tonight.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
